auto_fixes_applied.description: Jane Street package version constraint: (>= 0.16) → (>= v0.16). All ppx_inline_test/ppx_assert opam versions use the v prefix; without it, opam version comparison treats v0.17.1 < 0.16 and CI would fail at opam install --deps-only.
Same fix: >= "0.16" → >= "v0.16" for ppx_inline_test and ppx_assert.

Renamed misleading test 'find_tag_positions: ignores {unknown}' — the test actually verifies the tag IS found (find_tag_positions does not filter; filtering happens in parse). New name: 'find_tag_positions: returns all {tag} patterns including unknown ones'.

CMT quick-start showed `./arch-query /tmp/self.db exported` on an architecture-schema DB. arch-query's exported subcommand queries `WHERE exported=1` but the architecture DB has column `exposed` (not `exported`). Replaced with `sqlite3 /tmp/self.db "SELECT count(*) FROM functions;"` which works with the actual schema.

Corrected false claim that arch-query and arch-load call bin/arch_index_cli. arch-query is a self-contained bash script calling sqlite3 directly; arch-load is a self-contained Python 3 script. Only arch-index delegates to bin/arch_index_cli.

Added actionable error message on golden file diff failure: 'Golden file mismatch — update: see docs/adr/001-self-index-golden.md'. Without this, CI showed only 'files differ' with no guidance on how to fix.

findings.summary: Schema column naming inconsistency: architecture DB uses `exposed`, arch-query subcommand is named `exported` and queries `exported=1`
findings.evidence: architecture-schema.sql defines `exposed BOOLEAN`. arch-query line 108: `WHERE exported=1`. The two are incompatible — arch-query was designed for the comment_db LSP schema, not the full architecture schema. This is a pre-existing issue surfaced by the new documentation that shows both tools together.
findings.fix: Out of scope for this PR (pre-existing architectural debt). Track separately. The README and docs/install.md now avoid showing arch-query commands against architecture-schema DBs.
